# criblio_search_sources (Data Source)

SearchSources DataSource

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "criblio_search_sources" "my_searchsources" {
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Read-Only

- `items` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items))

<a id="nestedatt--items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items`

Read-Only:

- `auth_tokens` (Attributes List) Optional bearer or shared tokens accepted by this source. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items--auth_tokens))
- `cribl_api` (String) Absolute path for Cribl HTTP API requests (for example <code>/cribl/_bulk</code>). Used when <code>type</code> is <code>cribl_http</code>.
- `description` (String) Optional description shown in configuration UIs.
- `disabled` (Boolean) When true, the source does not accept new connections.
- `elastic_api` (String) Absolute path for Elasticsearch bulk API requests. Used when <code>type</code> is <code>elastic</code> (for example <code>/elastic/_bulk</code>).
- `host` (String) Bind address for the listener.
- `id` (String) Source configuration id.
- `port` (Number) TCP port when the source type uses a single port field. Must be a valid listener port (1–65535). Each port may be used by at most one Local Search source at a time for the same host and protocol: the same port cannot be shared across different sources or different <code>type</code> values. In Cribl Cloud, the UI only lists ports from the workspace pre-provisioned pool (the set varies by environment; Splunk HEC commonly defaults to 8088). Ports already in use by another source, or outside the pool, are rejected by the API—pick an available port from the UI or API.
- `prometheus_api` (String) Absolute path for Prometheus remote write requests. Used when <code>type</code> is <code>prometheus_rw</code> (for example <code>/write</code>).
- `splunk_hec_acks` (Boolean) When true, enable Splunk HEC acknowledgements. Used when <code>type</code> is <code>splunk_hec</code>.
- `splunk_hec_api` (String) Absolute path for Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC) API requests. Required when <code>type</code> is <code>splunk_hec</code> (for example <code>/services/collector</code>).
- `subscriptions` (Attributes List) WEF subscriptions; required when <code>type</code> is <code>wef</code>. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items--subscriptions))
- `tcp_port` (Number) TCP listen port when the source type uses a dedicated TCP port (for example <code>syslog</code>).
- `tls` (Attributes) TLS listener settings for Local Search sources (paths often use cloud substitution tokens). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items--tls))
- `type` (String) Protocol discriminator selecting the ingest source protocol and listener shape.
- `udp_port` (Number) UDP listen port when applicable (for example <code>syslog</code> or <code>prometheus_rw</code> when required by the deployment).

<a id="nestedatt--items--auth_tokens"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items.auth_tokens`

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) Optional label for this token.
- `enabled` (Boolean) When false, the token is rejected.
- `token` (String) Secret token string clients must present.


<a id="nestedatt--items--subscriptions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items.subscriptions`

Read-Only:

- `batch_timeout` (Number) Seconds to collect events before sending to Cribl.
- `compress` (Boolean) Receive compressed events from the source.
- `content_format` (String) Content format for delivered events.
- `heartbeat_interval` (Number) Maximum seconds between endpoint check-ins before marking unavailable.
- `id` (String)
- `locale` (String) RFC 3066 locale for Windows clients (e.g. en-US).
- `metadata` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items--subscriptions--metadata))
- `queries` (Attributes List) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--items--subscriptions--queries))
- `query_selector` (String)
- `read_existing_events` (Boolean) Send previously existing events for new subscriptions.
- `send_bookmarks` (Boolean) Track received events for resume after re-subscription.
- `subscription_name` (String) Subscription name exposed to Windows clients.
- `targets` (List of String) DNS names of endpoints that forward these events (wildcards allowed).
- `version` (String) Version UUID for this subscription; changes when subscription parameters change.
- `xml_query` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--items--subscriptions--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items.subscriptions.metadata`

Read-Only:

- `name` (String)
- `value` (String) JavaScript expression to compute field's value, enclosed in quotes or backticks. (Can evaluate to a constant.)


<a id="nestedatt--items--subscriptions--queries"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items.subscriptions.queries`

Read-Only:

- `path` (String)
- `query_expression` (String)



<a id="nestedatt--items--tls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `items.tls`

Read-Only:

- `cert_path` (String) Filesystem or cloud path to the certificate PEM.
- `disabled` (Boolean) When true, TLS is not terminated on this listener.
- `min_version` (String)
- `priv_key_path` (String) Filesystem or cloud path to the private key PEM.
